title: Fix PyPI upload regression in semantic-release reusable workflow
description: Reusable semantic-release workflow reports success but PyPI artifacts are not uploaded for consuming repos after migration. Reinstate explicit, reliable artifact upload to PyPI in reusable workflow with safe guards and logs.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented fix in .github/workflows/semantic-release-reusable.yml: added explicit twine upload of dist/* for released builds, plus warning when PyPI token is missing. YAML parse check passed locally. Next: run workflow lint, commit, push, and monitor CI.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Adjusted workflow for GitHub Actions compatibility: removed secrets usage from step if-expression and moved token check into shell logic. This avoids workflow validation/startup failures while preserving explicit twine upload behavior.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Code fix is pushed and PR opened: https://github.com/AnthusAI/platform-ci/pull/10 (base=main). Merge is blocked by ruleset requiring CODEOWNERS review from AnthusAI/corporate-overlords. Waiting for required review before merge to main.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Adjusted PR flow to match Git Flow: retargeted PR #10 base from main to develop. Next step is merge into develop, then open promotion PR develop->main.
